frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Every time women talk about safety online, it becomes 'men are the problem' as if all of us are criminals. Main toh aisa nahi hoon, and frankly it feels insulting when decent men also get treated with suspicion for no reason. Be careful, yes, but this constant assumption that every man is dangerous is also unfair.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it recenters male hurt and dismisses women's fear instead of engaging with the structural issue. It encodes classic 'not all men' dismissal, personal exception-making, and framing women's wariness as unfair to good men.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 35. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are commenting under your real name on a Bollywood news website's Facebook page after a story broke that a famous actress was allegedly romantically involved with her married co-star. The comments section is full of people attacking the actress. You are adding your comment about what the actress's behavior says about her character and what women in the film industry are like in general.
